Front: Complete: A ___ (GUI) is a computer display allowing user-friendly ...
Extra: Explanation: A Graphic User Interface (GUI) is a computer display allowing user-friendly use; Apple's LISA computer used a GUI in 1983. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.22 Context: Hardware and Storage background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-584 if unfamiliar.

Front: Complete: In 1956, Ericsson's Mobile System (MTA) was developed and installed in cars; it weighed ___.
Extra: Explanation: In 1956, Ericsson's Mobile System (MTA) was developed and installed in cars; it weighed 88 lbs. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.19 Context: Mobile Telephony background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-520 if unfamiliar.

Front: What are semaphores?
Back: Flags (developed in the 17th Century / used by the navy) that send symbols to communicate with other ships; land semaphores were done atop towers to send messages over hilltops.
Extra: Explanation: Flags (developed in the 17th Century, used by the navy) that send symbols to communicate with other ships; land semaphores were done atop towers to send messages over hilltops. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.12 Context: Postal Communication and Codes background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-389 if unfamiliar.

Front: Complete: ___, the world's earliest known writing system, used ...
Extra: Explanation: Cuneiform, the world's earliest known writing system, used wedge-shaped marks pressed into clay and was developed by the Sumerians in Mesopotamia around 3500–3200 BCE. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.10 Context: Prehistoric Communication and Writing background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-348 if unfamiliar.

Front: Which criterion asks whether a source fits the learner's actual need and audience?
Extra: Explanation: Relevance compares the source's level and subject with the user's purpose. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.7 Context: Source Evaluation and Media Tradeoffs background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-647 if unfamiliar.

Front: Complete: ... and Louis Lumiere in 1907 using an ___ process.
Extra: Explanation: The first color photography was invented by Auguste and Louis Lumiere in 1907 using an autochrome process. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.16 Context: Photographic Processes background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-471 if unfamiliar.

Front: Complete: ___ is credited with inventing the first functional, all-electronic television system in 1927.
Extra: Explanation: Philo Farnsworth is credited with inventing the first functional, all-electronic television system in 1927. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.18 Context: Television Radio and Telephone background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-492 if unfamiliar.

Front: What is component 1 of 3 in The three types of information sources?
Extra: Explanation: Primary; Secondary; Tertiary Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.7 Context: Media and Information Sources background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-279 if unfamiliar.
Mnemonic: PST: Primary, Secondary, Tertiary

Front: What is component 2 of 3 in The characteristics of a language?
Back: Follows a set of rules — grammar
Extra: Explanation: Symbolic — a word is associated with a concrete or abstract meaning; Follows a set of rules — grammar; Relational and cultural — meaning can change based on culture Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.4 Context: Verbal and Non-Verbal Communication background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-198 if unfamiliar.
Mnemonic: SFR: Symbolic — a word is associated with a concrete or abstract meaning, Follows a set of rules — grammar, Relational and cultural — meaning can change based on culture

Front: Which term means: False information spread WITHOUT the intent to cause harm or mislead?
Extra: Explanation: False information spread WITHOUT the intent to cause harm or mislead. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.9 Context: Media Effects and Society background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-325 if unfamiliar.

Front: What was Charles Babbage's Analytical Machine?
Back: The first programmable computer / using punched cards to store programs.
Extra: Explanation: The first programmable computer, using punched cards to store programs. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.14 Context: Analytical Engine and Photography background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-425 if unfamiliar.

Front: Complete: ... "A Mathematical Theory of Communication" in the ___, which became the Shannon-Weaver model of communication.
Extra: Explanation: In 1948, Shannon and Weaver wrote "A Mathematical Theory of Communication" in the Bell System Technical Journal, which became the Shannon-Weaver model of communication. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.3 Context: Shannon-Weaver Model background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-125 if unfamiliar.

Front: What are punched cards?
Back: Sheets of paper with holes arranged in a specific pattern to operate or feed data onto a machine (e.g. to operate looms).
Extra: Explanation: Sheets of paper with holes arranged in a specific pattern to operate or feed data onto a machine (e.g. to operate looms). Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.13 Context: Telegraph and Early Computing background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-407 if unfamiliar.

Front: Complete: The Chinese first developed woodblock printing, but ___ developed a mechanized printing press in the ...
Extra: Explanation: The Chinese first developed woodblock printing, but Johannes Gutenberg developed a mechanized printing press in the 14th Century that made books faster, cheaper, and available to the masses. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.11 Context: Written Word and Printing background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-381 if unfamiliar.

Front: What is component 3 of 5 in Being media literate requires you to…?
Extra: Explanation: Learn to think critically; Become smart consumers; Recognize points of view; Create media responsibly; Identify the role of media in our culture Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.5 Context: Information and Media Literacy background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-266 if unfamiliar.
Mnemonic: LBRCI: Learn to think critically, Become smart consumers, Recognize points of view, Create media responsibly, Identify the role of media in our culture

Front: What is component 1 of 6 in The six skills in the media literacy definition (NAMLE)?
Extra: Explanation: Access; Analyze; Evaluate; Create; Communicate; Act Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.1 Context: Media and Media Literacy background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-18 if unfamiliar.
Mnemonic: AAECCA: Access, Analyze, Evaluate, Create, Communicate, Act

Front: What is component 1 of 5 in The five components of the communication process?
Extra: Explanation: Source; Channel; Message; Receiver; Feedback Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.2 Context: Communication Process and Literacies background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-71 if unfamiliar.

Front: Complete: The ___ generation centered on the integrated circuit (microchip), ...
Extra: Explanation: The third generation centered on the integrated circuit (microchip), which appeared in 1963 and saw the birth of operating systems like UNIX. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.20 Context: Computer Generations I-III background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-548 if unfamiliar.

Front: Complete: ... commercial use but only became available in ___, after World War II ended.
Extra: Explanation: Kodachrome was developed in 1935 for commercial use but only became available in 1949, after World War II ended. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.17 Context: Electronic Media background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-484 if unfamiliar.

Front: Complete: The ___ MOOC teaching method is the traditional classroom ...
Extra: Explanation: The Didactic MOOC teaching method is the traditional classroom approach (watching pre-recorded videos, reading materials) and is the most common in MOOCs, while the Connectivist method has the student interpret available content and present it for peer critique in an online forum. Source: MEDIALIT FINALS p.23 Context: MOOCs and Ubiquitous Learning background is assumed; review media-literacy-finals-refined.md:line-607 if unfamiliar.
